I would get much more aggressive on a pullback to the $16-17 range, which would put the forward P/E closer to 9x on my 2027 estimates and offer a better risk-reward for a name this dependent on gold-price leverage. For now, shares up 63% over the past year make me patient. But if you are underweight high-grade Canadian gold, Wesdome belongs on the short list. And if a mid-tier ever comes calling with a premium bid, buyers at current prices should still do just fine.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Depressed Equities That May Be Worth Investigating Further
data.description:
Investment Thesis

Over the last year, some companies have been crushed due to various internal or external factors that may be overblown. Below are a list of companies with relatively strong or appealing valuations at their current price levels that may warrant further investigation to see if you should take a small speculative position on a potential turnaround. Given that these companies have experienced massive selloffs recently, investor sentiment is near all time lows and the momentum may be working against you for the foreseeable future. Further, these investments may be value traps, but they could also rebound for a nice return once sentiment shifts or the companies start performing better in their underlying businesses. I believe when investors are running for the exit in equities, there's a chance you can find a great company at a decent price for longterm capital appreciation.

PayPal Holdings Inc., (PYPL)

Market Cap: $44.76B

Total Debt: $12.3B

Cash on hand: $10.42B

PYPL has been beaten down over the last couple of years, with large declines following some earnings reports, and faces many headwinds with their underlying business that has worsened investor sentiment significantly. As of April 24th, they are down about 15% YTD and 81% on a 5 year basis. They currently hold a "sell" quant rating but a "buy" for analysts on Seeking Alpha. PYPL recently underwent management changes with the ousting of their last CEO and this is a turnaround play. However, there is activist interest, large cash positions, and a large buyback program which may help find a bottom in this stock.

Earnings are upcoming at the beginning of May and I expect the company to continue struggling in the short-term while the new CEO who started in March gets acquainted. However, I will be looking for commentary on Venmo and how they have continued to make inroads with the college aged adults who use Venmo regularly. I believe this is still their most attractive avenue of growth and profitability moving forward. Further, they've recently started to grow their accessibility by allowing Venmo and PayPal users to transact with each other, which was surprisingly not a capability before.

Lululemon athletica inc. (LULU)

Market Cap: $16.38B

Total Debt: $1.8B

Cash on hand: $1.81B

LULU has also been beaten down over the last couple of years and has significantly underperformed the market over the last year. However, they currently hold a buy rating among Seeking Alpha analysts and a Hold quant rating with roughly a hold rating overall based on the above factor grades. I started following this company in October of 2024, where I gave them a buy ratting based on strong underlying fundamentals, and continued my buy rating in January 2025 after the stock rose 33% in the short-term. So far, this stock has significantly underperformed over short-term, but there's potential that they will be able to turn this around.

They recently appointed a new CEO who will start in September, but investors have remained pessimistic about the company's outlook. I believe that the company will continue to struggle over the next 12-18 months at least, while we wait for the changes to take place with new management. However, the company beat top and bottom line expectations in March, have an approved $1.2 billion available for share repurchases, and have enough cash on hand to cover their debt entirely. Further, if we look at their profitability factor grade of an A, they are outperforming their 5 year averages in all categories, such as return on common equity, return on assets, and gross profit margin. Again, I believe investors still have pain ahead in the short-term, but this company may find a bottom during this turn around process.

Novo Nordisk A/S (NVO)

From a valuation perspective, NVO has a better overall value profile than both PYPL and LULU.

Market Cap: $171.16B

Total Debt: $20.61B

Cash on hand: $5.82B

Currently, NVO has a hold Quant rating on Seeking Alpha, while they have a B+ in their valuation factor grade and an A+ in their profitability factor grade. Looking at the company from a value investor's lens, we see that their forward P/E ratio is currently 11.17, their forward EV/EBITDA ratio is 8.74, and their forward price to cash flow is 12.74 which are all lower than the sector averages. Further, they've provide a 4.88% trailing twelve month dividend yield, with a forward yield of 4.68% and a payout ratio of just over 50%. However, given the recent headwinds this company has faced, the stock has been hammered.

NVO has faced many headwinds during the last 12-18 months including intense competition in their obesity/weight loss drug, litigation, and negative news reports. Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) and NVO have been battling for market share in the obesity/weight loss drug space, which was supposed to be NVO's next big avenue of growth. However, as of April 24th, the sentiment between NVO and LLY has started to drastically shift, after reports of higher growth in Wegovy scripts over the last week than LLY's Zepbound. They've also had problems with Hims & Hers (HIMS) where there was the potential for litigation, but this has recently resolved leading to a positive outcome for NVO and HIMS. Further, they have run into warnings from the FDA, which has contributed to negative sentiment in the stock as well. Overall, this is also a turnaround story, with a lot of the negative sentiment that has been built into the price of the stock already reaching solutions without much more of a headache for investors. I believe this stock has potential over the next 12-18 months for a rebound from their current price levels and I will look for commentary on market share changes in their upcoming earnings report in May.

Risks

There are significant risks to investing in companies that have experienced recent share price declines and face headwinds moving forward. As most of these companies have underperformed the broader market, investors may continue to pull away from these companies in the future if other bad news circulates. Further, the market has been very volatile during the last couple of years, and some companies have been hit harder than others when bad news arises.

Another risks to these investments is the idea of catching a falling knife. Although some of companies look attractive after massive sell-offs, such as NVO, others have experienced the sell-off due to inherent risks in their business model, like PYPL and LULU, which may present a riskier investment than anticipated and could be viewed as a value trap.

Finally, previous stock outperformance does not guarantee that the companies mentioned in this article will recover. Even though these companies have experienced times of market outperformance, or present strong fundamentals in some areas, sentiment has shifted due to inherent concerns in their underlying business models that investors should focus on moving forward as this may affect the investment thesis. Further, when modeling a fair value price for these companies, investors should be cautious with using a lower required return than what's shown with the CAPM chart below, which accounts for their risk relative to the market.

[object HTMLElement]

Conclusion

In conclusion, when good companies that have many daily consumers have significant declines, I tend to track and follow their underlying business performance looking for value while others run to the exit. When looking for these discounted companies, the momentum may continue to work against you in the short-term, but there's a chance for long-term outperformance which is an attractive risk versus reward scenario in some cases. Investors should perform their own research and develop their own opinions on the companies mentioned before making any investment decisions. Investors should also be cautious of "catching a falling knife" in these types of investments as it's very difficult, if not impossible, to time the bottom accurately.

[object HTMLElement]
